top_insights: School teaches compliance; true wealth is built by self-taught outliers who ignore the syllabus.
Nobody is coming to save you. The moment you realize this is when you actually start winning.
Stop networking when you need something. Build relationships when you have nothing to gain.
Your narrative is your leverage. If you don't tell your story, someone else will write it for you.
Walking isn't just exercise; it's the cheapest, most effective strategy to solve complex business problems.
hook_patterns: Personal anecdotes establishing authority or unique experience
emotional_drivers: Validation (seeking confirmation of one's views or status)
Aspiration (desire for wealth, success, and leverage)
Security (need for stability and clear takeaways)
emerging_narrative: A cultural shift toward radical self-reliance and the validation of individual success stories, where audiences resonate most with content that demystifies wealth, challenges conventional wisdom, and offers actionable, high-status advice.

viral_examples.author: Codie_Sanchez
viral_examples.text: I have a friend who used to work for 2 billionaires after all he's seen he said to me, "I wish you not one penny over $299 million. Reality gets completely lost somewhere after that." I think about that sometimes.

viral_examples.author: naval
viral_examples.text: Elon’s stance is principled. Trump’s stance is practical. Tech needs Republicans for the present. Republicans need Tech for the future. Drop the tax cuts, cut some pork, get the bill through.

viral_examples.author: naval
viral_examples.text: The smartest people are all self-taught, even if they went to school.

viral_examples.author: naval
viral_examples.text: Panic led to lockdowns. Lockdowns led to fiscal stimulus. Stimulus led to inflation. Inflation led to monetary tightening. Tightening leads to recession. The panic wasn’t free - and the bill is coming due.

viral_examples.author: thedankoe
viral_examples.text: Normalize not having an opinion on things you don’t understand.

winning_patterns: Personal anecdotes establishing authority or unique experience
